title: Athlete's Foot
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Athlete's foot, or tinea pedis, is a contagious fungal infection of the feet.
It often starts between the toes and can cause itching, burning, cracked, or scaly skin.
Prevention and care include keeping feet clean, dry, and cool and avoiding barefoot exposure in public wet areas.
Complicated disease, bacterial infection, diabetic foot risk, or nail involvement needs clinician-directed handling.
